free_text: Charge dispersion studies of heavy-mass elements in the
fission of Th-232 by protons of medium energy
Observations about charge dispersion curves
as a function of single and multi-chance fission.
A target stack consisted of three thorium foils,
thickness 0.005 cm each,and three copper foils (the
copper foils were 0.025 cm thick),the latter placed
upstraem with respect to the thorium foils. All six
foils were carefully aligned,wrapped with aluminum
foils (0.002 cm thick).
.NaI(Tl)crystal detector.
.2 cm**3 Ge(Li) semiconductor detector.
Errors were estimated taking into account:
* random errors associated with decay-curve analysis,
* chemical yields,
* photopeak area determination, as well as the
systematic errors associated with detector
efficiencies,
* spread in proton beam energy,etc.
Errors were estimated by taking the square root of
the sums of the squares of the individual errors and
were found to be 10-20% in most cases.
Duplicate results agreed usually to 5%, occasionally
to 15%,except in the case Ce-144 which occasionally
were slightly greater. However cross sections
calculated in the case of the timed double-extraction
experiments showed poorer reproducibility (in some
cases the values differed by as much as 50%).
This indicates how the errors may be propagated
by the exponential relations.
Tbl.2,3 from J.Inorg.Nucl.Chem.,35(1973)3991
monitor is corrected.
